title: Smoky Pillowy Irish Seafood Chowder
description: A hearty and energizing Irish dish featuring smoky seafood flavors and a pillowy texture, without eggs.

ingredients.name: Smoked Haddock Fillets
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use haddock with a high smoke level for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Irish Butter
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality Irish butter for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Leeks
ingredients.notes: Make sure to slice the leeks thinly to distribute the flavor evenly.

ingredients.name: Potatoes
ingredients.notes: Choose high-starch potatoes for the best result.

ingredients.name: Ocean-Salted Cod
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use cod with a high ocean-salt content for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika is the key to the smoky flavor in this dish.

ingredients.name: Fresh Parsley
ingredients.notes: Use fresh parsley for the best flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic finely to distribute the flavor evenly.

ingredients.name: Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: Use freshly squeezed lemon juice for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Sea Salt
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Creme Fraiche
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality creme fraiche for the best flavor and texture.

instructions: Melt the Irish butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced leeks and cook until they are softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced potatoes and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the smoked haddock fillets, ocean-salted cod, smoked paprika, garlic, lemon juice, sea salt, and black pepper to the pot. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Pour in the seafood stock and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Stir in the creme fraiche and cook for another 2-3 minutes, or until the mixture is heated through.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the chowder hot, garnished with fresh parsley.
Let the chowder rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
